steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B
steps.content:
At $t=0$, choice B gives $A(0)=31{,}971.93$, which does not match the initial balance of $36{,}100$.
Thus choice B fails.

steps.title: Fails. Testing choice C
steps.content:
At $t=0$, choice C gives $A(0)=31{,}971.93$, again not equal to the initial $36{,}100$.
Thus choice C also fails.

steps.title: Fails. Testing choice A
steps.content:
At $t=0$, choice A gives $A(0)=36{,}100$, matching the initial balance.
Evaluating at $t=13$: $$36{,}100(1.05)^{13}\approx 68{,}071.93$$, which equals the given balance after 13 years.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
